opportunities.rationale: FCX at $59.53 (FCX Investor Relations, May 19 2:20pm ET). Copper producer with strong fundamental demand from AI/data-centre infrastructure and EV transition. Materials sector XLB at $50.22 recovering from -5.84% WTD dip. Non-structural shock — sector caught in defensive rotation, not copper-specific breakdown. RSI recovering from oversold. Invalidated below $52 if copper prices collapse below $4/lb or China demand weakens structurally.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on confirmed hold above $58 after XLB stabilizes above $50.50; confirm FCX above 20d MA.

opportunities.rationale: NUE at $221.74 (MarketWatch May 19 real-time). Steel demand underpinned by US infrastructure spending; ISM Manufacturing 52.7 in April (4th straight expansionary month). Analyst consensus PT $212.44 (public.com) — stock trading above PT indicating momentum. Non-structural shock from sector rotation. RSI recovering. Invalidated below $208 if PMI data surprises sharply to downside.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on flash PMI data May 21 if above 50; confirm NUE holds above 50d MA.

opportunities.rationale: APD at $293.31 (CNN Markets last close). Industrial gases company with hydrogen energy exposure and multi-year infrastructure capex programme. Analyst consensus PT $313.45 (public.com, 11 analysts, Buy) implying 7%+ upside from current. Sector selling pressure not fundamentals-driven. Invalidated below $275 if industrial gas demand shows structural weakness.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter in $288-295 zone after XLB stabilizes; watch May 21 PMI for sector read.

opportunities.rationale: XLB at $50.22 (MarketWatch May 19 close). Recovering from -5.84% WTD dip. Sector rotation driven by defensive bid, not materials-specific structural breakdown. Bullish broad market regime (S&P above all MAs) creates mean-reversion opportunity. LOW conviction — wait for first confirmed daily close above $51.50 before adding. Invalidated if Flash PMI (May 21) shows manufacturing contraction below 50.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Wait for first confirmed daily close above $51.50 after PMI data; do not enter before May 21.

opportunities.rationale: STLA crashed 25% on Feb 6 2026 (Economic Times) after disclosing $26.5B EV restructuring charge. Stock at $7.41 (Morningstar May 18; intellectia.ai May 18). 52-week range $7.03-$13.14. 17 Wall St analysts with median PT $8.25 (tickernerd) implying 11.5% upside. Q1 2026 earnings beat estimates Apr 30 but stock fell 10% on €960M adjusted operating loss disclosure (Quartz). European listing STLAM at €6.41 (investing.com May 18). Non-structural shock — €22B write-down is a one-time restructuring charge; core automotive operations and Jeep/Ram brand portfolio intact. CEO replacement underway stabilising management. Invalidated below $6.20 (52-week low area) if EV transition losses accelerate further.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter $7.00-$7.50 on stabilization after Q1 selloff; watch for management commentary on restructuring completion timeline. Hold 30 days for mean-reversion toward $8.25 analyst median.
